description: Optimal u32 (DEHL) operations for Z80, verified
register_convention: u32 stored in DEHL: D=byte3(MSB), E=byte2, H=byte1, L=byte0(LSB)

shl32.notes: ADD HL,HL=11T, EX DE,HL=4T, ADC HL,HL=15T, EX DE,HL=4T

sar32.notes: SRA preserves bit 7 of D (sign bit)

add32_stack.description: DEHL += [SP] (second operand on stack: low16 on top, high16 below)
add32_stack.notes: Stack must have low16 on top. Consumes 4 bytes from stack.

sub32_stack.notes: OR A clears carry before first SBC

neg32.notes: LD A,0 (not XOR A) preserves carry between bytes

sext16_32.notes: RLA gets sign bit into CY, SBC A,A materializes 0xFF or 0x00

xor32_ixiy.notes: No native 16-bit XOR, must do byte-by-byte

rotr32.description: DEHL = ROTR(DEHL, 1) (rotate right through all 32 bits)
rotr32.notes: 32T if no wrap, 40T if carry wraps to bit 31. Branch variant.

_hlhl_convention: HLH'L' alternative u32 layout: HL (main bank) = low 16-bit, H'L' (shadow via EXX) = high 16-bit. Frees DE for pointer arithmetic. EXX does not disturb flags (CY survives across EXX).

hlhl_add32.description: HLH'L' += BCB'C' (BC=low 16 addend, B'C'=high 16 addend in shadow)
hlhl_add32.notes: ADD HL,BC=11T, EXX=4T, ADC HL,BC=15T (shadow B'C' now in BC), EXX=4T. EXX preserves CY — ADC correctly propagates carry from low to high. Advantage over DEHL: two independent 16-bit addends in BC/B'C'; DE remains free for pointer use.

hlhl_shl32.description: HLH'L' << 1 (logical shift left, doubles the u32 value)
hlhl_shl32.notes: Same cost as DEHL shl32 (34T). ADD HL,HL=11T doubles low 16; EXX=4T; ADC HL,HL=15T doubles high 16 with carry from low; EXX=4T. Identical pattern to DEHL but uses EXX instead of EX DE,HL.

hlhl_load_nn.notes: LD HL,nn=10T each, EXX=4T each. Total 44T. For runtime values: LD HL,(addr) = 16T each → 48T total.

hlhl_vs_dehl._doc: When to prefer HLH'L' over DEHL
prefer_hlhl_when: Function needs DE for pointer (e.g., LDIR, screen address via DE)
32-bit add has two genuinely independent 16-bit halves (avoids 54T stack path)
Multiple u32 variables: DEHL uses all 4 main GPR pairs; HLH'L' leaves DE+BC free
prefer_dehl_when: No EXX-zone conflict with other shadow register usage
Need INC/DEC on individual bytes (INC D, INC E — no shadow equivalent without EXX)
hlhl_vs_dehl.key_constraint: HLH'L' requires exclusive EXX-zone: cannot mix with other EXX usage (e.g., loop counter in B'C'). Shadow is a shared resource — all 6 shadow regs swap simultaneously.
